title: Getting started
description: Install FDS, drop the theme stylesheet into any page, and style your first component with design tokens.

blocks.text: FDS ships as a single npm package: `flexa-design-system`. It contains a design-token source (DTCG JSON), pure TypeScript functions that turn tokens into CSS, and two pre-built stylesheets for projects that don't want a build step at all.

The fastest way to use FDS is the pre-built theme stylesheet. It defines every `--fx-*` custom property (light + dark + reduced-motion) and nothing else — no classes, no resets, no specificity fights.
Then style anything with the semantic variables:
Dark mode is one attribute — set `data-fx-scheme="dark"` on `<html>` (or let `prefers-color-scheme` do it automatically):
Everything is a pure function over the token registry — emit a theme stylesheet at build time, resolve token ids into `var()` strings, or gate a custom theme against the WCAG pairs:

rows: h1–h6/body element rules driven by the variables
The DTCG token source — feed it to Style Dictionary etc.

blocks.text: `base-typography.css` scopes its rules to `[data-fx-type="flexa/root"]` — add that attribute to your content wrapper to opt in. This keeps FDS from restyling pages that only want the variables.
Next: read how the three token tiers fit together in the Token tiers guide, or browse every token on the Tokens page.
